title: Build staff authentication and appointment viewing system

requirements.text: Create a staff login page with username and password input fields where all staff members can authenticate using a single shared set of hardcoded credentials

quotes: Authentication method is username and password
No need of any storage. Just only one password and user id for all staff members

acceptanceCriteria: Staff login page displays username and password input fields
Single hardcoded username and password stored in backend
Login form validates credentials against hardcoded values
Error message displays when incorrect credentials are entered
Successful login redirects to appointments dashboard

requirements.text: Implement backend authentication endpoint that validates staff credentials against a single hardcoded username and password combination

quotes: No need of any storage. Just only one password and user id for all staff members

acceptanceCriteria: Backend function accepts username and password parameters
Credentials are compared against hardcoded values in Motoko code
Returns success response when credentials match
Returns error response when credentials do not match
No database or complex storage required

requirements.text: Create an appointments dashboard page that displays all appointments and is only accessible to authenticated staff members

quotes: After login they should see appointment

acceptanceCriteria: Appointments dashboard page displays list of all appointments
Page is only accessible after successful staff login
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login page
Dashboard shows appointment details including patient information, date, time, and status

requirements.text: Implement session management to maintain staff authentication state across page reloads and navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Staff remains logged in after page refresh
Authentication state persists across browser tabs
Staff can navigate between pages without re-authenticating
Session expires after a reasonable timeout period

constraints: Use single-actor Motoko architecture for backend
Do not create database or complex storage for credentials
Maintain existing React with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS frontend stack
Do not modify immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Multiple staff accounts with different credentials
Admin interface for managing staff accounts
Role-based access control with different permission levels
